generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T AGARICUS PROCERUS. A. procerus, Scop. (Parasol mushroom.) Found in England, common in pasture (also in Pennsylvania). Stem, 8-12 inches high. Esculent . Authority. Poisoning cases. ► Furious delirium, with frantic cries and vehement resistance to remedies, followed by a state like delirium tremens. ► Insensibility. ► Giddiness and staggering, as if intoxicated. ► Pupils contracted; dilating as sensibility returns. ► Occasional convulsive spasms. ► Debility. ► Drowsiness.
